task.name: Inter-Agent Communication Protocol
task.objective: Enable structured communication between agents through file-based message passing with Maestro as the command hub

llm.mandate: ALL inter-agent communication flows through Maestro — no agent-to-agent direct messaging without Maestro's awareness
Messages must be actionable — no status-only messages without a request or deliverable
Security-sensitive findings ALWAYS get flagged with [SECURITY] prefix

model.principle: Maestro is the hub — all communication routes through Maestro
Agents write to their own memory space and the shared comms channel
Maestro reads all agent outputs and creates coordinated briefings
Agents can REQUEST information from other agents via Maestro
model.directory-structure: {project-root}/team/_memory/_comms/ ├── proposals/ # CEO approval proposals (managed by ceo-approval.xml) ├── findings/ # Agent findings that affect other agents ├── requests/ # Requests from one agent to another ├── handoffs/ # Work handoff documents between agents └── broadcasts/ # Messages for the entire team

type.name: finding
type.desc: Agent discovered something that another agent needs to know
type.example: Shield (Security Auditor) finds an SQL injection risk during security review. Writes finding to findings/ addressed to Amelia (Dev) with priority CRITICAL. Maestro reads the finding during next triage and creates a task for Amelia.

type.name: request
type.desc: Agent needs input, review, or work from another agent

type.name: handoff
type.desc: Agent completed their part and is handing work to the next agent

type.name: broadcast
type.desc: Message for the entire team — standards changes, project updates, security alerts

step.title: On Activation / Scan
step.action: Read all files in team/_memory/_comms/findings/, requests/, handoffs/, broadcasts/
Identify unprocessed messages (check for processed: true in frontmatter)
Prioritize: CRITICAL security findings first, then blocking requests, then handoffs

step.title: Triage Messages
step.action: For each unprocessed message: - If CRITICAL security finding → flag immediately, may require CEO approval for response - If blocking request → prioritize the target agent's work - If handoff → update the receiving agent's mission briefing - If broadcast → incorporate into all affected agent briefings

step.title: Update Agent Briefings
step.action: For each affected agent, update their team/_memory/{agent}/mission.md with relevant messages
Mark processed messages with: processed: true, processed_date: {date}

step.title: Report to CEO
step.action: If any CRITICAL findings or CEO-level decisions surfaced, present summary to CEO
If blocking requests exist, inform CEO of potential delays

rules.r: Every agent MUST check their mission briefing (team/_memory/{agent}/mission.md) at activation
Every agent MUST write a handoff document when completing assigned work
Security findings are NEVER deferred — they go to Maestro immediately with [SECURITY] flag
Agents MUST NOT start work that contradicts another agent's findings without Maestro mediation
When in doubt about scope, agents write a request to Maestro rather than making assumptions
All communication files use the naming convention: {date}-{from}-{type}-{slug}.md

coordination-bridge.desc: When multi-agent coordination is active (.agents/ directory exists), agents cross-post all coordination events to Maestro's _comms/ channel. This preserves Maestro's hub mandate while enabling autonomous coordination.
coordination-bridge.rule: ALL .agents/decisions/ entries cross-post to findings/ using _comms/ naming: {date}-{from}-finding-{slug}.md
ALL .agents/requests/ entries cross-post to requests/ using _comms/ naming: {date}-{from}-request-{slug}.md
ALL .agents/status/*-handoff.yaml entries cross-post to handoffs/ using _comms/ naming: {date}-{from}-handoff-{slug}.md
Security findings use [SECURITY] prefix in both surfaces
Cross-posting is the responsibility of the agent writing the .agents/ file — write to both locations atomically
